steps.title: Use a direct contrast
steps.content: The task asks for a difference between surface-dwelling and cave-dwelling tetras. The notes state the clearest contrast: surface-dwelling tetras can see, while cave-dwelling tetras have lost that ability.

steps.title: Reject noncomparative choices
steps.content: Choice A says the populations are different but does not specify how. Choice B discusses only cave-dwelling tetras. Choice C merely identifies the two populations. The remaining choice directly contrasts their ability to see. The answer is D.
